common.pdfLabel: Click here to download the supporting PDF for this evaluation

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Present each letter to the student and ask: "What is the name of this letter?" then "What sound does this letter make?"

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Say the name of each letter to the student and ask them to write it.

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Assess the student's ability to identify rhymes, segment/blend, and count syllables. For each section, ask the student to listen carefully and respond.

rhyming.title: Identify rhyming words
rhyming.prompt: Ask the student: "Do these words rhyme?"

segmenting.title: Segment and blend syllables
segmenting.prompt: Read the segmented word. Ask the student: "What word does that make?"

syllableCounting.title: Count syllables in a word
syllableCounting.prompt: Say the word to the student. Ask: "How many syllables do you hear in this word?" The student should tell you the number.

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Assess reading simple syllables and sound-letter correspondence. For the correspondence section, show 3 letters to the student and ask them to point to the letter that makes the indicated sound.

syllableReading.title: Read CV, VC and CVC syllables
syllableReading.prompt: Ask the student to read each syllable.

soundCorrespondence.title: Match sounds to corresponding graphemes
soundCorrespondence.prompt: Show 3 letters to the student. Say: "Point to the letter that makes the sound /___/." For example, show "b – f – l" and ask: "Point to the letter that makes the sound /f/."

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Evaluate the student's ability to read open/closed syllables, complex syllables, and high-frequency words.

openClosedSyllables.title: Read open and closed syllables
openClosedSyllables.prompt: Ask the student to read each syllable aloud.

complexSyllables.title: Read complex syllables - CVCC, CCVC
complexSyllables.prompt: Ask the student to read each complex syllable aloud.

frequentWords.title: Read 5 high-frequency words
frequentWords.prompt: These words should be recognized instantly. Ask the student to read each word quickly.

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Ask the student to identify sounds at the beginning and end of words. Say the word clearly and ask: 'What sound do you hear at the beginning/end?'

beginningSound.title: Identify the sound at the beginning of a word

endingSound.title: Identify the sound at the end of a word

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Evaluate the student's ability to read word families (word variations), words with frequent endings, and high-frequency words.

wordFamilies.title: Read and recognize word families
wordFamilies.prompt: Ask the student to read all words in each family and observe if they recognize the connection between them.

frequentEndings.title: Read words with frequent endings: -s, -es, -er, -ez, -ait, -aient
frequentEndings.prompt: The student must read each word pronouncing the ending correctly.

frequentWords.title: Read 5 high-frequency words
frequentWords.prompt: Ask the student to read each word aloud.

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Ask the student to read sentences aloud respecting punctuation and intonation, then read pseudo-words to assess decoding skills.

sentenceReading.title: Read 3 repeated sentences with fluency
sentenceReading.prompt: Ask the student to read each sentence several times to improve fluency.

pseudoWords.title: Improve speed and accuracy
pseudoWords.prompt: Ask the student to read these pseudo-words (invented words) to assess their decoding skills.

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Evaluate the student's ability to read words containing advanced graphemes (ail, eil, euil, ouil). Ask the student to read each word aloud identifying the target sound.

advancedGraphemes.title: Read 10 words containing: ail, eil, euil, ouil
advancedGraphemes.prompt: Each grapheme must be read correctly within the context of the word.

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Ask the student to visualize the story. Say: "Close your eyes and imagine what you hear while I read." Read the text aloud, then ask the 5 comprehension questions.

comprehension.title: Answer 5 comprehension questions
comprehension.prompt: After listening to the story, have the student answer these questions to assess their understanding.

instructions.title: Instructions:
instructions.body: Ask the student to read a short 4-5 sentence text independently, then ask 5 comprehension questions.

readingFluency.title: Read text independently
readingFluency.prompt: Ask the student to read the text aloud on their own.

comprehension.title: Answer 5 comprehension questions
comprehension.prompt: After reading, have the student answer these questions to assess their understanding.

notRequired.title: Unit marked as not required
notRequired.description: This unit has been excluded based on the teacher's professional judgment and will not affect reports.

units.unit1-6: Workshop 6: Identification of initial and final sounds
units.unit2-1: Workshop 7: Reading simple syllables and sound-letter correspondence
units.unit3-1: Workshop 8: Reading open, closed, and complex syllables
units.unit6-1: Workshop 11: Word families and common endings
units.unit9-1: Workshop 14: Reading and comprehension of short text
